zotero.abstractNote: High-density extracellular recordings provide a rich source of information about the electrical activity of neurons. However, inferring the underlying biophysical parameters of multi-compartment Hodgkin-Huxley (HH) models from these recordings remains a significant challenge due to the high dimensionality of the parameter space and the computational cost of simulating these models. We propose a simulation-based inference (SBI) framework to identify the parameters of multi-compartment HH models from high-density extracellular voltage recordings. Our approach leverages a neural posterior estimator to learn the mapping from extracellular voltage traces to the posterior distribution over biophysical parameters. We demonstrate that our method can accurately recover the parameters of multi-compartment models in synthetic data and show that it can be applied to experimental recordings from retinal ganglion cells. Our framework provides a scalable and efficient tool for characterizing the biophysical properties of neurons from extracellular recordings.
